|

Friend, I know you keep hearing about this so-called "Carolina Comeback." Let's look at some facts: Today, 55 counties have fewer jobs than before the great recession and 78 counties have more people looking for work than they did during the great recession. Our median income is 41st in the nation. And do I really need to mention Governor McCrory's discriminatory legislation has lost the state thousands of jobs and hundreds of millions of dollars. **[It's time for a leader that moves our state and our economy forward. If you're with Roy, add your name here.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202613/2fde7c66/400169ba/156a741a/865752047/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-on-jobs?utm_campaign=adv_jobs2_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em16_160715_adv_gq_jobs2_ful__mj&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")** This week, Roy released a jobs plan that will ensure more money ends up in the pockets of middle class families. name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: Today only, we'll send you a free button with each donation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
say it loud and proud | Friend --

This week, when Bernie and Hillary united in New Hampshire, we showed the world what our team stands for: We’re going to raise wages and make college and health care a reality for all Americans. We’re going to close the wage gap and fight systemic racism and discrimination in all its forms, because we believe our country is stronger when we all rise together.

But at the Republican convention in just three days, Donald Trump and the Republicans will tell a very different story.

They’ll talk about building walls and banning Muslims, making it harder to vote and easier to buy guns.

They'll attack Hillary, they'll disparage President Obama, and basically spend the whole week saying things aimed at dividing us up and turning us against each other -- because that’s their plan to win.

Our plan is different: We'll show that we're stronger together. And we know that love will always trump hate.

**If you’re proud to be a part of this team, we want you to show it. That’s why, today only, we’ll send you a free “Love trumps hate” button with every donation of $10 or more:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjU5OTE0OTIS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMjE4MjU2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

The work we’re doing here really matters -- for our country, for our future. Let us know where to send your button so you can show the world you’re a part of this team.

Thank you,

body:
Trump and Pence | Friend --

Donald Trump just announced Mike Pence will be his running mate.

If there was any doubt about Trump's intentions -- if you ever thought maybe he was just grandstanding about his anti-equality, anti-woman, anti-immigrant policies and his commitment to an economy that favors millionaires and corporations over working families -- well, with Pence as his vice president, we know exactly what to expect.

This choice shows us just how Trump would govern and what kind of America he would shape. Going up against Trump and Pence reinforces that this election is bigger than just a decision between two political parties.

It's about who we are as a nation. Are we the kind of country that denigrates women, immigrants, or anyone that looks, loves, or believes differently than us? Do we close our doors to people whose diverse viewpoints will only better us? Do we let nativism, bigotry, and intolerance become inscribed in our nation's character? Will we let "me-first" economics determine our families' paths to success?

This is a critical moment in this race -- and a chance to show exactly which side you're on.

**If you’ve been waiting for the right time to make your first donation, this is it. Chip in right now to take a stand and we’ll send you a sticker to say thanks:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjU5OTI5ODUS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQyMjI3OTg2S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks,

Christina

subject: Your Weekly Update
body:
| | | Team Scott's Week In Action
---
[View this email in your browser](http://us13.campaign-archive2.com/?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b74c630cd3&e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| Greetings,
---
| Policy was high on the agenda this week, as Phil continued to demonstrate strong leadership, further detailing his approach to critical issues facing Vermonters, and elaborating on his plans for tackling those issues. A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below.

**DEEP DIVE INTO THE ECONOMY, OUR SHRINKING WORKFORCE, BUDGET PRIORITIES & MORE**

Phil was interviewed this week for the Burlington Free Press’ “The Table,” giving Vermonters the opportunity to get a strong sense of Phil’s leadership style, approach to governing and priorities for Vermont as Governor.

During the in-depth video interview, which you can view [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a1f50bfd3f&e=19cebfe173), Phil elaborates on his experience and how he’ll leverage that experience as Governor to **strengthen our economy, make Vermont more affordable and restore faith and trust in state government**. He also dives into a number of specific issues, including:
* **Demographic challenges** \- “If we have 30,000 less [working class Vermonters] helping to bring in revenue to the state and spreading the burden out a bit, it puts the burden on the rest of us who are still standing. What I see as the solution is to try and grow the economy, try to revitalize and restore it. It’s not firing on all cylinders here. What we need to do is to restore that so we can bring revenue in organically, rather than forcing that through taxes and fees.”
* **Legislative priorities –** “I came to the conclusion that we weren’t focusing enough on the economy. I brought that back to the legislative leaders and to the Governor. I said that we should really focus the next two to four legislative years on just trying to do something about the economy. **_Just focus on that_**. Every single committee could have a piece of this. Whether it’s Education with public private partnerships; whether its Transportation with infrastructure; whether its Ways and Means with tax incentives. _Every single committee_ could have a piece of this, if we all focused and agreed to pull in the same direction.”
* **Retaining and attracting young workers** – “We need to offer opportunity in some way. They need jobs – they need competitive jobs that pay a competitive salary. We also have to have affordable housing...We do a poor job of bringing about affordable housing for the working class, and that’s where we need to focus. And on providing some incentives, as well, for them to stay here.”
* **Responsible budgeting -** “Over the last six years, all we’ve done is raise taxes and fees and we’ve increased our spending. Then we have a deficit _every single year after_ and yet the legislature is surprised...We shouldn’t be growing [the budget] any faster than the economy or wages in the previous year. That’d be another thing that I’d advocate for.”

Hear more from Phil on these issues, and others, by listening to the full interview [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=15a9186faa&e=19cebfe173) and reading more [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f4fdab2529&e=19cebfe173).
***
**A LEADER WITH INTEGRITY: PHIL’S LATEST VIDEO MESSAGE**
Phil has run a campaign based on the issues and his vision for leading Vermont. He **continued his positive message** this week by releasing a video urging civility and respect in this election. In contrast to the misleading negative attacks of his primary opponent, **Phil has invited Vermonters to join his campaign to “make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children.”**

View the official press release and full video [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=61cf864dfd&e=19cebfe173).
| [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U1swrSa04gY)
---

If you share the video on social media, use the hash tag, #theVermontway

***

**CHALLENGING THE GOVERNOR ON HIS POOR EB-5 OVERSIGHT AND TRANSPARENCY**
Yet again, Vermonters had to learn new information on the EB-5 investigation from reporters, rather than from the Governor himself. [Vermont Public Radio](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=989248598c&e=19cebfe173) reported Thursday that the Shumlin Administration allowed Jay Peak’s owners to continue to solicit investors months after the federal Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) launched its investigation.

Phil issued a statement expressing disappointment that “the Shumlin Administration enabled the owners of the EB-5 projects in the Northeast Kingdom, who are accused of multiple counts of fraud, to continue to solicit investors for months after the SEC had suspended that permission for Jay Peak. Even after repeatedly failing to provide compliance information to state regulators, they were allowed to raise more money. **By the Administration’s own admission, it was a ‘calculated risk.’ Yet, they’ve not yet explained why they took this risk or why they allowed the problem to continue to grow.** ”

While Phil has consistently supported these projects as a useful economic development tool, he has always cautioned that **there must be proper oversight** , which was clearly not the case under Governor Shumlin’s leadership.

He added, “In order to grow the economy, **we need leaders who will _capably and transparently oversee programs_ and put regulatory responsibility ahead of politics**. This is essential to **restoring faith and trust in state government** , the integrity of Vermont’s regulatory system and the value of this economic development program.”

Read the official statement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3cb197ae3e&e=19cebfe173).

***

**FORUMS, FESTIVALS, MEETINGS AND MORE PARADES**
At an **AARP Town Hall** , Phil was excited to hear from attendees and discuss the issues impacting our aging population and how its effecting our state and our country. At a picnic and forum hosted by the **Washington County Republican Party** Phil further **distinguished himself from his opponent** , reinforcing his commitment to **prioritize the economy** and his plans for improving it. For example, Phil discussed how to **make Vermont more predictable and stable for businesses, including ending the constant threat of regulations and policies that stifle business such as the “carbon tax.”**

Team Scott also made time to meet with Vermonters at Montpelier’s **Cody Chevrolet** , the **Rutland Chamber Mixer** , and the **Casella “Truck Rodeo** ,” and swung by **Bradford** for a parade! Thanks to all who joined us at these great events!

Photo Credit:@PhilScott4VT

Don’t miss us next week in [Orange County](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ac26c9fae9&e=19cebfe173) and at [Joe’s Pond](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=34af2e520d&e=19cebfe173), or at the [VCRD/VPT Gubernatorial Forum](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=56e8991434&e=19cebfe173), [Channel 17/Town Meeting TV LIVE Candidate Forum](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5f97f0a865&e=19cebfe173), and [Veterans Appreciation Jamboree](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b9b3d2facc&e=19cebfe173). View our Events page [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=49d8f9f7b8&e=19cebfe173) for all upcoming events.

***

**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Integrity Matters_**
“Lt. Gov. Phil Scott cares about the well-being of all Vermonters and has demonstrated over his entire life the inherent trait of doing for others, which is the hallmark of those who are genuinely motivated by public service.” Thomas Joseph, Wilmington (Source: Times Argus: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e1f9c60230&e=19cebfe173))

**_Addressing our Demographic and Health Care Issues_**
**“** Republican Phil Scott said the cost of services would be spread more evenly if the state’s population grew from 630,000 to 700,000 people. On health care, he advocated eliminating Vermont Health Connect, the state website set up to offer health coverage required under federal law, and join the federal exchange or hook up with another state that has been successful.” (Source: VT Digger: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=73d143be0f&e=19cebfe173))

**_On Shumlin’s Mismanagement of the EB-5 Investigation_**
“Whatever their reasons for allowing the scheme to continue while under federal investigation, the fact that journalists had to dig up this information further undermines faith and trust in state government.” (Source: Vermont Business: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9b907aeb86&e=19cebfe173))

***

**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_SBEC: Best to Worst State Tax Systems for Entrepreneurship and Small Business_**
The Small Business and Entrepreneurial Council released its annual ranking of state’s business climates. The score is a composite of a number of different measures that together reflect how hospitable a state is to businesses. **Vermont ranked 44 th overall, and worst among New England states. **We tout these lists when Vermont ranks highly for quality of life. But it is difficult to balance the two, and we clearly have not budgeted with both in mind for the last six years. **As Governor, Phil will make the economy and affordability the top priorities in Montpelier**. Read the full report [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=db7fb2c57e&e=19cebfe173) and read more on Phil’s proposals to strengthen our economy for working families and Vermont businesses [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=df66f6cb70&e=19cebfe173).

*******

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3f660b35b7&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f390f0cbf8&e=19cebfe173).

clean_body: | | | Team Scott's Week In Action | | | Greetings, | Policy was high on the agenda this week, as Phil continued to demonstrate strong leadership, further detailing his approach to critical issues facing Vermonters, and elaborating on his plans for tackling those issues. A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below. DEEP DIVE INTO THE ECONOMY, OUR SHRINKING WORKFORCE, BUDGET PRIORITIES & MORE Phil was interviewed this week for the Burlington Free Press’ “The Table,” giving Vermonters the opportunity to get a strong sense of Phil’s leadership style, approach to governing and priorities for Vermont as Governor. During the in-depth video interview, which you can view , Phil elaborates on his experience and how he’ll leverage that experience as Governor to strengthen our economy, make Vermont more affordable and restore faith and trust in state government. He also dives into a number of specific issues, including: Demographic challenges \- “If we have 30,000 less [working class Vermonters] helping to bring in revenue to the state and spreading the burden out a bit, it puts the burden on the rest of us who are still standing. What I see as the solution is to try and grow the economy, try to revitalize and restore it. It’s not firing on all cylinders here. What we need to do is to restore that so we can bring revenue in organically, rather than forcing that through taxes and fees.” Legislative priorities – “I came to the conclusion that we weren’t focusing enough on the economy. I brought that back to the legislative leaders and to the Governor. I said that we should really focus the next two to four legislative years on just trying to do something about the economy. Just focus on that. Every single committee could have a piece of this. Whether it’s Education with public private partnerships; whether its Transportation with infrastructure; whether its Ways and Means with tax incentives. Every single committee could have a piece of this, if we all focused and agreed to pull in the same direction.” Retaining and attracting young workers – “We need to offer opportunity in some way. They need jobs – they need competitive jobs that pay a competitive salary. We also have to have affordable housing...We do a poor job of bringing about affordable housing for the working class, and that’s where we need to focus. And on providing some incentives, as well, for them to stay here.” Responsible budgeting - “Over the last six years, all we’ve done is raise taxes and fees and we’ve increased our spending. Then we have a deficit every single year after and yet the legislature is surprised...We shouldn’t be growing [the budget] any faster than the economy or wages in the previous year. That’d be another thing that I’d advocate for.” Hear more from Phil on these issues, and others, by listening to the full interview and reading more . * A LEADER WITH INTEGRITY: PHIL’S LATEST VIDEO MESSAGE Phil has run a campaign based on the issues and his vision for leading Vermont. He continued his positive message this week by releasing a video urging civility and respect in this election. In contrast to the misleading negative attacks of his primary opponent, Phil has invited Vermonters to join his campaign to “make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children.” View the official press release and full video . | If you share the video on social media, use the hash tag, #theVermontway CHALLENGING THE GOVERNOR ON HIS POOR EB-5 OVERSIGHT AND TRANSPARENCY Yet again, Vermonters had to learn new information on the EB-5 investigation from reporters, rather than from the Governor himself. reported Thursday that the Shumlin Administration allowed Jay Peak’s owners to continue to solicit investors months after the federal Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) launched its investigation. Phil issued a statement expressing disappointment that “the Shumlin Administration enabled the owners of the EB-5 projects in the Northeast Kingdom, who are accused of multiple counts of fraud, to continue to solicit investors for months after the SEC had suspended that permission for Jay Peak. Even after repeatedly failing to provide compliance information to state regulators, they were allowed to raise more money. By the Administration’s own admission, it was a ‘calculated risk.’ Yet, they’ve not yet explained why they took this risk or why they allowed the problem to continue to grow. ” While Phil has consistently supported these projects as a useful economic development tool, he has always cautioned that there must be proper oversight , which was clearly not the case under Governor Shumlin’s leadership. He added, “In order to grow the economy, we need leaders who will capably and transparently oversee programs and put regulatory responsibility ahead of politics. This is essential to restoring faith and trust in state government , the integrity of Vermont’s regulatory system and the value of this economic development program.” Read the official statement . FORUMS, FESTIVALS, MEETINGS AND MORE PARADES At an AARP Town Hall , Phil was excited to hear from attendees and discuss the issues impacting our aging population and how its effecting our state and our country. At a picnic and forum hosted by the Washington County Republican Party Phil further distinguished himself from his opponent , reinforcing his commitment to prioritize the economy and his plans for improving it. For example, Phil discussed how to make Vermont more predictable and stable for businesses, including ending the constant threat of regulations and policies that stifle business such as the “carbon tax.” Team Scott also made time to meet with Vermonters at Montpelier’s Cody Chevrolet , the Rutland Chamber Mixer , and the Casella “Truck Rodeo ,” and swung by Bradford for a parade! body:
They have their eyes on Washington. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTIwOTI2MgS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMjEyMTc0S0&mt=1&rt=0) | | ****_" The Republican Governors Association will do everything we can to have Bill Bryant win for Governor."_****
---
\- Susana Martinez, Chair of the Republican Governors Association, June 2016
Dear Friend,
In 2014, the Republican Governors Association made that same promise in Massachusetts and in Maryland. They turned both of those states from blue to red. **We know what this means: We need to do everything we can to win.**
And this week? This week, while the RGA has their sights set on us, **our opponent launched his first TV ads.** The timing couldn't be worse: ballots drop in the next few days for the primary -- the only major electoral test for our campaign before November.
Our opponent wants to win this race with TV ads, so we're fighting back with everything we've got: not only with ads, but with a digital push, and most importantly, by building our campaign from the grassroots on up by bringing on six field organizers to drive our primary election Get-Out-The-Vote push.
This is what it comes down to: It's our grassroots organizing versus the RGA and their millions in outside spending. It's our grassroots organizing versus our opponent, who just bought ads across the state.
[**We 're going to fight back in every way we can, with our grassroots efforts at the core. Will you make sure we're prepared for the RGA and other attacks by contributing $5 today toward our $10,000 pre-primary goal?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=OTIwOTI2MgS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=OTYxMjEyMTc0S0&mt=2&rj=OTYxMjEyMTc0S0&rt=0)
Mark my words -- when this election is in the books, it's going to be the strength of our organizing that defines our success. That's why your support, right now, is so important.
Thank you for your support of this campaign.
Aisling Kerins
Jay Inslee for Washington
